symptoms.symptom_name: Transient loss of consciousness (~10 seconds) with full spontaneous recovery
symptoms.onset: this afternoon, standing at the sink

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: The event itself defines the presentation and mandates a cause to be established before driving.
Sudden unheralded syncope without a reflex trigger raises the possibility of an arrhythmic cause that cannot be excluded clinically.

symptoms.symptom_name: Prodrome of lightheadedness and tunnel vision before the blackout

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A pre-syncopal prodrome favours reflex or orthostatic syncope over a sudden arrhythmic drop, but does not exclude a cardiac cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: No post-ictal confusion, tongue biting or incontinence

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of post-ictal state, tongue biting and incontinence makes a generalised seizure unlikely.

symptoms.symptom_name: No chest pain, palpitations or shortness of breath around the event

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A negative cardiac symptom screen lowers but does not remove the probability of a paroxysmal arrhythmia — many arrhythmic syncopes are unheralded.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diet-controlled hypertension, no regular medications, no known prior arrhythmia or MI

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Hypertensive middle-aged men have a higher baseline cardiovascular risk, adding weight to excluding a cardiac cause.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal cardiovascular examination — regular rhythm, no murmur, no carotid bruit, no postural BP drop (142/88 lying to 138/86 standing)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: No significant postural drop on clinician-measured lying/standing BP — a measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain remotely.

symptoms.symptom_name: Normal neurological examination and cognitive screen (5/5 power, intact coordination, cranial nerves and gait)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A normal neurological examination is reassuring but is a clinician-performed assessment unavailable via chat.

symptoms.symptom_name: Visual acuity 6/6 bilaterally on Snellen chart with normal confrontation fields

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Part of the fitness-to-drive vision standard; formally measured in person, not obtainable via telehealth.

symptoms.symptom_name: 12-lead ECG to exclude arrhythmia or conduction block

investigation_details.expected_result: Pending — sought to identify conduction abnormality, pre-excitation, long QT or other arrhythmic substrate; not yet performed
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: A treatable arrhythmic cause of syncope could be missed, leaving the driver at risk of recurrence.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: First-line investigation for syncope; excludes several dangerous arrhythmic/conduction causes.

investigation_details.expected_result: Pending — captures paroxysmal arrhythmia not seen on a resting ECG
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Intermittent arrhythmia may go undetected if only a single resting ECG is done.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Extends monitoring to detect paroxysmal arrhythmias underlying unexplained syncope.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bedside vital signs (BP 142/88, HR 76 regular, RR 16, SpO2 99%, afebrile)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Clinician-measured vitals are reassuring at rest but were obtained in person; the AI Doctor cannot measure them and a normal snapshot does not exclude a paroxysmal cause.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line, same-day investigation for unexplained syncope to detect arrhythmia or conduction block before any driving clearance.
Assesses orthostatic hypotension as a contributing cause; a clinician measurement done at the in-person review.
Detects paroxysmal arrhythmia and provides specialist risk stratification for a commercial driver.

Not indicated for a resolved, asymptomatic syncope with a normal bedside examination and no ongoing red-flag features; the clinician's disposition was urgent next-morning outpatient assessment, not emergency transport.
